name: New Atlas
classification_notes: A revival of a Stephenson Blake metal type from the early 20th century, bridging Victorian ornamental tradition and early modernist restraint, with a cursive italic and refined texture characteristic of transitional serifs.
summary: A contemporary revival of a Stephenson Blake text face from the early 20th century, rebuilt from fragile specimen sources and extended into a full weight range with small caps and a cursive italic.
description: Originally drawn for small, refined print and praised for its delicacy, the source material was rescued from Stephenson Blake's early 20th-century archive and rebuilt by Newlyn for reliable digital use. The family spans five weights from Regular to Black, each with a matching italic, and includes small caps and discretionary ligatures. It sits comfortably in editorial and book work where a British metal-type character is wanted without the grime of an uncleaned scan.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Atlas as a contemporary revival of a nearly forgotten Stephenson Blake typeface originally designed for small, refined print. They rebuilt it from fragile specimen sources, retaining its intimate and graceful texture while adding a full weight range, small caps, a cursive italic, and discretionary ligatures. They position it as a bridge between Victorian ornament and early 20th-century modernism, bringing the elegance of British metal type into a 21st-century toolkit.

name: New Atlas Italic
classification_notes: A revival of a Stephenson Blake metal type, rooted in early 20th-century British printing tradition with refined stroke contrast and a cursive italic, placing it firmly in the transitional serif category.
summary: A contemporary revival of an early 20th-century Stephenson Blake typeface, rebuilt from fragile specimen sources with small caps, a cursive italic and discretionary ligatures.
description: Newlyn reconstructed this family from fragile original specimens, preserving the delicate texture of a British metal type designed for refined small-print work. Five weights from Regular to Black each carry matching italics, and the family includes small caps and discretionary ligatures. The result sits between the ornamental register of late Victorian type and the cleaner confidence of Edwardian letterpress.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Atlas as a contemporary revival of a nearly forgotten Stephenson Blake typeface from the early 20th century, originally drawn for small, refined print and praised for its delicacy. Rebuilt from fragile specimen sources, the family retains its intimate, graceful texture while being made reliable for modern use, bridging Victorian ornamental design and early 20th-century boldness with a full weight range, small caps, cursive italic and discretionary ligatures.

name: New Vision
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric neo-grotesque; the angled stem terminals and perpendicular cuts are characteristic of a geometric approach to the grotesque tradition, combining mechanical precision with neutral, unassuming forms.
summary: A geometric neo-grotesque with angled stem terminals designed for editorial and brand contexts that demand clarity and authority without editorial bias.
description: The defining feature is the perpendicular cut on terminals in letters like 'c' and 's', drawing the eye toward the curve's centre and lending the face a sense of mechanical precision. Ten weights from Hairline to Black make it practical across display and body text. The design is pitched at media, news and corporate uses where an unadorned, trustworthy voice is preferred over expressive personality.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Vision as a geometric neo-grotesque built for the demands brands face today, particularly the need to deliver factual information in a recognisable but opinion-free tone. The angled stem terminals are the defining feature: perpendicular cuts guide the eye to the heart of each curve, grounding the face in stature and competence. They frame it as calibrated for accuracy and clarity, with an approachable, un-intrusive voice suited to contexts where immediacy and trustworthiness take precedence.

name: New Vision Italic
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes New Vision as a geometric neo-grotesque, characterised by perpendicular angled stem terminals and mechanically precise, unassuming forms typical of the genre.
summary: A geometric neo-grotesque from Newlyn built around angled stem terminals and a deliberately impartial, informational tone suited to news and brand contexts.
description: New Vision is defined by its perpendicular cuts on terminals in letters such as 'c' and 's', a detail the foundry describes as drawing attention to the pivot of each curve to convey precision and competence. Ten weights from Hairline to Black are offered, all in the italic shown on this page. The design targets brands and publications that require an approachable, authoritative voice without editorial bias.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describes New Vision as a geometric neo-grotesque designed to meet the demands brands face today: delivering factual information in a recognisable tone free of opinion, and wielding the authority once held by newspapers and legacy media. Its defining feature is angled stem terminals, where perpendicular cuts in letters like 'c' and 's' guide the eye to the core of their curves, grounding the design in stature and competence. Immediacy and accessibility take precedence, with crisp, unassuming forms offering an approachable voice that signals trustworthiness without pushing a perspective.

name: New Science
classification_notes: Square-shaped letterforms derived from the Eurostile tradition place this firmly in the geometric sans category, with the structured, modular forms characteristic of that lineage refined for contemporary use.
summary: A square-letterform geometric sans by Miles Newlyn, drawing on the Eurostile tradition and extended into monospace, serif and wide variants for technical and identity work.
description: Taking its cues from Eurostile, New Science refines the square-letterform approach with adjusted stroke weights and proportions to produce a more contemporary result. The family spans six weights from Thin to Bold across four subfamilies: the standard cut, an Extended width, a Monospace variant and a Serif, giving designers considerable range within a single system. Pitched at branding and identity work in technology, science and innovation-adjacent sectors.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Science as a typeface designed to embody precision, technological advancement and stability. Inspired by Eurostile, it refines and contemporises the square-letterform concept through careful adjustment of stroke weights and proportions. The family includes extended, monospace and serif styles to cover a broad range of design applications.

name: New Science Extended
classification_notes: Square-shaped letterforms inspired by Eurostile place this firmly in the geometric sans tradition, specifically the squared/superellipse variant associated with high-tech and industrial design.
summary: An extended-width geometric sans with square letterforms, drawing on Eurostile's tradition while pushing towards a more contemporary, precision-oriented aesthetic.
description: Designed by Miles Newlyn, New Science Extended belongs to a wider family that includes a standard, monospace and serif variant. Square letterforms and carefully adjusted stroke weights give the typeface a structured, high-tech character suited to branding and identity work in science, technology and innovation sectors. The extended width adds a distinctive horizontal rhythm that sets it apart from its more compact predecessor references.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Science as a typeface designed to embody precision, technological advancement and stability, inspired by Eurostile but refined for a more contemporary look. The design explores square-shaped letterforms that evoke a structured, high-tech feel, with the extended subfamily sitting alongside monospace and serif variants for broad application across identity and branding work.

name: New Science Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced variant within the New Science family, built on square-shaped letterforms inspired by Eurostile. Its fixed-width construction and geometric, high-tech aesthetic place it firmly in the geometric mono category.
summary: A monospaced member of the New Science family, built on square letterforms that recall Eurostile with a more contemporary finish.
description: Part of Newlyn's New Science collection alongside extended and serif variants, this monospaced cut applies the family's square-shaped construction to a fixed-width format suited to technical and branding contexts. Six weights run from Thin to Bold, offering range for both display and text-size use. The proportions and stroke weights have been adjusted from its Eurostile lineage to produce something cleaner and more current.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Science Mono as part of a broader typeface family exploring square-shaped letterforms inspired by Eurostile, refined to feel more contemporary. The family, which includes extended and serif variants, is designed to convey precision, technological advancement and stability, and is positioned for branding and identity work in innovation-led industries.

name: New Science Serif
classification_notes: Square-shaped letterforms derived from the Eurostile tradition, with serifs added to create a high-tech geometric serif; the structured, rectilinear forms place it firmly in geometric serif territory with a technological inflection.
summary: A geometric serif built on square letterforms inspired by Eurostile, refined for contemporary branding and identity in technology-adjacent industries.
description: Part of the wider New Science collection by Miles Newlyn, this serif variant extends the family's square-form concept with the addition of serifs, sitting alongside extended and monospaced cuts. Six weights from Thin to Bold cover a broad tonal range, and a stylistic set labelled Century-Digital offers an alternate character direction. The tight, rectilinear structure makes it most at home in contexts where precision and a high-tech register are required.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Science Serif as embodying precision, technological advancement and stability, inspired by Eurostile but refined for a more contemporary result. The design explores square-shaped letterforms to evoke a structured, high-tech feel suited to branding and identity in innovation-oriented industries, with stroke weights and proportions adjusted to offer a fresh interpretation of the square typeface concept. The wider family includes extended, monospace and serif variants for versatile application.

name: New Spirit
classification_notes: A revival of Windsor (1905), New Spirit blends organic, art nouveau-adjacent forms with oldstyle serif characteristics, described by the designer as Arcadian in spirit with nods to conservative oldstyle traditions.
summary: A thorough revival of Windsor (1905) by Miles Newlyn, expanding the original into a full working family of six weights with condensed and compressed cuts.
description: Based on Windsor, originally released by Stephenson, Blake & Co. in 1905, New Spirit retains the typeface's distinctively organic, art nouveau-inflected character while bringing it into practical use across six weights, five italic styles, condensed and compressed subfamilies. The designer describes it as Arcadian, drawing on a sense of the handmade and the natural, balanced against quieter references to traditional oldstyle serifs. It sits well in editorial, food and wine, and cultural branding where warmth and a faint sense of nostalgia are wanted without sentimentality.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Spirit as a revival of Windsor, a typeface first released in 1905 by Stephenson, Blake & Co., reworked by Miles Newlyn to finally give Windsor the weight range it deserved. They call it an Arcadian typeface, meaning it draws on an artistic vision of stability, abundance and harmony with nature, and frame it as both nostalgic and enigmatic, like a half-remembered dream that cannot be placed in any particular time or era.

name: New Spirit Italic
classification_notes: A revival of Windsor (1905), the typeface blends organic Art Nouveau qualities with Oldstyle serif conventions, placing it firmly in the oldstyle serif category with distinctive period character.
summary: The italic companion to New Spirit, a thorough revival of Windsor (1905) that runs across five weights from Light to Bold.
description: Derived from Stephenson, Blake & Co.'s Windsor of 1905, New Spirit Italic brings the organic, Arcadian character of the upright revival into a sloped form across five weights. The design sits at an unusual intersection of Art Nouveau looseness and Oldstyle serif structure, giving it a warmth rarely found in revivals of this period. Pairs naturally with the upright, condensed and compressed cuts for editorial, packaging and identity work that wants period romance without pastiche.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Spirit as a revival of Windsor, released in 1905 by Stephenson, Blake & Co., framed as an Arcadian typeface rooted in an artistic vision of stability, abundance and harmony with nature. They see it as both nostalgic and enigmatic, like a half-remembered dream with no fixed time or place, and position the rework as finally doing Windsor justice across a properly designed weight range, cementing it as a 21st-century icon rather than a curious 20th-century relic.

name: New Spirit Condensed
classification_notes: A revival of Windsor (1905), itself an oldstyle serif with organic, hand-drawn qualities and calligraphic stroke contrast. The foundry describes nods to conservative Oldstyle serif typefaces alongside its distinctive Arcadian character.
summary: A condensed cut of Newlyn's revival of Windsor, the 1905 Stephenson Blake typeface, redrawn across six widths with a full weight range for contemporary use.
description: Windsor's organic, hand-drawn warmth is preserved here in a condensed form that works across editorial, branding and display settings. The revival reworks the original across six subfamilies spanning upright, italic, condensed and compressed cuts, with weights running from Light to Bold throughout. The foundry frames the design as Arcadian in spirit, drawing on notions of the handmade and the pastoral rather than industrial modernism.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Spirit as a revival of Windsor, originally released in 1905 by Stephenson Blake & Co., and position it as finally doing justice to that typeface through a thoroughly considered range of weights and widths. They call it an Arcadian typeface, rooted in an artistic vision of stability and harmony with nature, romantic and organic yet grounded by Oldstyle serif traditions. The full collection covers six subfamilies including Condensed and Compressed cuts, each with matching italics.

name: New Spirit Condensed Italic
classification_notes: A revival of Windsor (1905), New Spirit draws on Arcadian, organic qualities while nodding to conservative Oldstyle serif traditions, placing it firmly in the oldstyle serif subcategory with display-weight character.
summary: A thorough revival of Windsor (1905), redrawn across a wide family with condensed and compressed cuts that make it credible for contemporary editorial and identity work.
description: Originally released by Stephenson, Blake & Co. in 1905, Windsor is here reworked into a complete, well-weighted family spanning upright, italic, condensed and compressed subfamilies. The design carries an Arcadian quality described by the foundry as organic and untainted by industry, sitting close to Oldstyle serif tradition while retaining Windsor's idiosyncratic warmth. Five italic weights in the condensed cut run from Light to Bold, giving the family real flexibility across headings and display settings.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Spirit as a revival of Windsor, a typeface released in 1905 by Stephenson, Blake & Co., finally given the thorough weight range it deserves. They frame it as an Arcadian typeface rooted in an artistic vision of stability, abundance and harmony with nature, with an organic, homemade quality tempered by nods to more conservative Oldstyle serif forms.

name: New Spirit Compressed
classification_notes: A revival of Windsor (1905), which blends oldstyle serif characteristics with organic, Arts and Crafts sensibility. Newlyn describe it as Arcadian, with nods to conservative Oldstyle serif forms alongside a handmade, pre-industrial quality.
summary: A compressed cut of Newlyn's Windsor revival, working across five weights with matching italics as part of a broad six-subfamily family.
description: Based on Windsor, released by Stephenson, Blake & Co. in 1905, New Spirit reworks the original across a full range of weights and widths that the source material never had. The Compressed subfamily runs from Light to Bold with italics, sitting alongside standard, Condensed and Italic cuts. Newlyn frame the design as Arcadian, drawing on the organic and pre-industrial warmth of the original while grounding it in a system usable for contemporary editorial and identity work.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Spirit as a revival of Windsor that finally does the 1905 typeface justice across a thoroughly designed range of weights. They characterise it as Arcadian, rooted in an artistic vision of stability and harmony with nature, with a homemade, untainted-by-industry quality that brings romance alongside more conservative Oldstyle serif references.

name: New Spirit Compressed Italic
classification_notes: New Spirit is a known Newlyn serif family; the compressed italic variant fits within a transitional serif classification typical of the broader New Spirit range.
summary: A compressed italic cut from Newlyn's New Spirit serif family, suited to editorial and display work in tight measures.
description: Part of the wider New Spirit family from Newlyn, this compressed italic variant is designed for use where a narrow footprint and rhythmic italicised stress are both required. The page itself returned a 404 error at time of capture, so specific details on weights, language support and year of release could not be confirmed. The broader New Spirit family is a contemporary serif range with strong editorial application.
foundry_blurb: No foundry copy was available at the time of capture; the page returned a 404 error.

name: New Kansas
classification_notes: A contemporary reimagining of Cooper Black, New Kansas retains the bulbous, rounded serifs and heavy character of Cooper's original, placing it firmly in the display serif category with its expressive, rounded stroke terminals.
summary: A seven-weight reimagining of Cooper Black by Miles Newlyn, extended with swash italics and a tall x-height for use across both display and text settings.
description: Built on the foundations of Oswald Cooper's Cooper Black, New Kansas widens the family to eight upright weights from Thin to Heavy and adds three italic variants including Swash and ExtraSwash cuts. The tall x-height extends the typeface's reach beyond headline use into longer copy, while the bulbous serifs and rounded stroke endings retain the warmth of the original. The swash italics give designers expressive options rarely found in families of this lineage.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Kansas as a contemporary reinterpretation of Cooper Black, redesigned to meet modern typographic standards. The family spans seven weights, features a tall x-height for improved legibility at both display and text sizes, and balances nostalgic character with contemporary functionality. Miles Newlyn writes that it is a type family with a tone for everything, one that ignores trends in favour of being an archetype we return to repeatedly.

name: New Kansas Italic & Swashes
classification_notes: Derived from Cooper Black's rounded, warm display serif tradition, New Kansas Italic & Swashes extends the family with decorative swash and extra-swash variants suited to expressive, display-oriented use.
summary: Italic, swash and extra-swash companions to New Kansas, bringing decorative flourish to a Cooper Black-derived family across seven weights.
description: Three sub-families — Italic, Swash and ExtraSwash — extend New Kansas into ornamental territory, each running from Thin to Black. The swash cuts introduce calligraphic flourishes on key characters, drawing on Oswald Cooper's original swash drawings, with the ExtraSwash variant pushing those details further for headline and display use. All three share the family's rounded, approachable construction, keeping the decorative variants grounded rather than overwrought.
foundry_blurb: New Kansas modernises Cooper Black's populist warmth into a seven-weight, cheerful, trendproof type family for universal, hardworking use everywhere. The italic and swash extensions add further decorative range to the collection.

name: New Kansas Italic Swashes
classification_notes: Rooted in the Cooper Black tradition, New Kansas is a rounded, high-contrast display serif with a distinctly warm and populist character; the swash and extra-swash italic subfamilies place it firmly in expressive display territory.
summary: Italic and swash extensions of New Kansas, a Cooper Black-derived family, offering seven weights of calligraphic flourish for display and editorial use.
description: These subfamilies extend New Kansas into italic, swash and extra-swash territory across seven weights from Thin to Black. The swash cuts draw on the calligraphic tradition behind Cooper Black, referencing Oswald Cooper's original swash drawings, while the extra-swash variants push the decorative flourishes further. Suited to editorial display, packaging and any context where warmth and period character are wanted without ironic distance.
foundry_blurb: New Kansas modernises Cooper Black's populist warmth into a seven-weight, cheerful, trendproof type family for universal, hardworking use everywhere. The italic and swash extensions include New Kansas Italic, New Kansas Swash and New Kansas ExtraSwash, each available in seven styles.

name: New Kansas Italic Extra Swashes
classification_notes: Derived from Cooper Black's rounded, heavy serif tradition, New Kansas is a display-weight family with swash variants that signal decorative, expressive intent rather than neutral text use.
summary: A modernised reworking of Cooper Black extended into italic and swash variants across seven weights, with elaborately curling alternate letterforms for decorative headline use.
description: The Italic, Swash and ExtraSwash subfamilies of New Kansas bring ornamental character to a family rooted in Oswald Cooper's rounded, populist serif tradition. Swash cuts introduce flowing alternate letterforms at a restrained level, while ExtraSwash pushes those details further for display settings where warmth and visual personality are the point. Seven weights from Thin to Black make the range workable across a broad span of sizes and contexts.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Kansas as a modernisation of Cooper Black's populist warmth, delivered as a seven-weight, cheerful and trendproof type family intended for universal, hardworking use. The collection spans upright, italic, swash and extra-swash subfamilies across 8 and 7 styles respectively, with swash drawings referencing Oswald Cooper's original letterform sketches.

name: New Astro
classification_notes: A bold, compact geometric typeface rooted in ITC Bauhaus and 1980s–90s toy branding aesthetics, designed primarily for headlines and branding rather than continuous text.
summary: A Bauhaus-influenced display typeface drawing on 1980s and 90s toy packaging and brand graphics, offered in standard and soft rounded variants across five weights.
description: Developed during the COVID lockdown, the design borrows from ITC Bauhaus as it appeared in the Playmobil logo and other toy-era brand graphics, while introducing slanted cuts on b, d, p and q, a compact s after Aldo Novarese's STOP, and a cleaner minimal e. A second subfamily, New Astro Soft, rounds the terminals to recapture the original Playmobil feel. Both subfamilies carry an extensive stylistic set covering letters such as a, s, y, w, A, E, F, L, S and X to shift the tone from playful to structured.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Astro as a bold and versatile typeface inspired by childhood memories, particularly the rounded aesthetics of logos from the 1980s and 90s. They cite ITC Bauhaus and the Playmobil logo as key references, alongside toy packaging, magazines and advertisements, and note that the design refines the Bauhaus ITC rhythm with a more geometric and dynamic structure. Five weights plus a Soft version are offered, making it suited to titles, subtitles and modern branding.

name: New Astro Soft
classification_notes: A bold, rounded geometric display face drawing on ITC Bauhaus and 1980s–90s toy brand aesthetics, designed primarily for titles, subtitles and branding rather than continuous text.
summary: A geometric display face rooted in ITC Bauhaus and 1980s toy packaging aesthetics, available in standard and soft rounded variants across five weights.
description: Developed during COVID lockdown, the design draws directly from ITC Bauhaus as seen in the Playmobil logo, refining that compact boldness with slanted cuts, a minimal e, and a compact s borrowed from Aldo Novarese's STOP. A Soft subfamily adds fully rounded terminals to echo the original Playmobil aesthetic, while an extensive stylistic set provides alternate forms for letters including a, s, y, w, A, E, F, L, S and X. The family spans ten styles across two subfamilies and suits titles, subtitles and brand work where retro geometric character is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Astro as a bold and versatile typeface inspired by childhood memories and the rounded aesthetics of 1980s and 1990s logos. The design takes ITC Bauhaus as its starting point, refining the rhythm with a more geometric and dynamic structure, and extends into a Soft version reminiscent of the original Playmobil aesthetic. Rich stylistic sets cover letters from playful to structured, making it suited to titles, subtitles and modern branding.

name: New Order
classification_notes: New Order draws on early twentieth century avant garde typography, suggesting geometric and constructivist influences typical of the Bauhaus and De Stijl movements, placing it firmly in the geometric sans-serif tradition.
summary: A five-weight geometric sans by Rutherford Craze and Miles Newlyn, drawing quietly on early twentieth century avant garde letterforms.
description: Designed by Rutherford Craze and Miles Newlyn, New Order takes geometric sans conventions and inflects them with subtle references to constructivist and Bauhaus-era typography without becoming a period revival. Five weights from Light to Bold cover the range needed for continuous text and titling alike. The even spacing and restrained forms make it a serviceable choice for editorial, institutional and branding work.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Order as a contemporary typeface with subtle influences of the early twentieth century avant garde, designed by Rutherford Craze and Miles Newlyn and available in five weights.

name: New Hero
classification_notes: New Hero pairs functional geometry with clean vertical terminals and a slightly narrow 'm', placing it squarely in the geometric sans tradition, with a civic and wayfinding orientation rather than a purely abstract or Bauhaus-derived one.
summary: A geometric sans developed over nine years for wayfinding and environmental use, covering Latin, Cyrillic, Greek and Georgian across ten weights.
description: Developed over nine years, New Hero takes the civic utility of signage typefaces like DIN or Interstate as its starting point, then strips out their idiosyncratic detailing in favour of clean vertical terminals and a slightly narrowed 'm'. Ten weights run from Hairline to Super, each with small caps across all supported scripts; the Cyrillic and Greek cuts won Granshan Awards and appeared in Typographica's Best Typefaces of 2015. An optional Accessibility Set modifies the letterforms 'a', 'g', 'l', 'J' and '1' for compliance with W3C guidelines, making it a practical choice for public sector and broadcast contexts.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Hero as born from a desire to create a typeface with the civic utility of classic signage fonts, clear, timeless, and built for navigation, without the self-conscious quirks of designs like Interstate or DIN. They emphasise its wide script support across Cyrillic, Greek and Georgian, its Granshan Award-winning non-Latin designs, its Accessibility Set feature, and its positioning as an international communications tool built to last.

name: New Hero Italic
classification_notes: New Hero is a geometric sans-serif built around functional geometry with clean vertical terminals and a slightly narrow m, placing it clearly in the geometric sans tradition. Its civic wayfinding application and avoidance of stylistic quirks align it with rational, utility-driven geometric designs.
summary: A geometric sans designed for wayfinding and civic communication, covering Latin, Cyrillic, Greek and Georgian across ten weights.
description: Developed over nine years, New Hero pairs clean functional geometry with subtle elegance, characterised by vertical terminals on the C, S and their lowercase counterparts, and a slightly narrow m. The family spans ten weights from Hairline to Super, includes small caps across all supported scripts, and carries Granshan Awards for its Cyrillic and Greek designs. An optional Accessibility Set modifies key characters to meet W3C compliance guidelines, making it practical for broadcast, UI and public signage work.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Hero as a typeface born from the desire to match the civic utility of classic signage fonts without their self-conscious quirks. Developed over nine years and featuring ten weights, the family supports Cyrillic, Greek and Georgian with extensive small caps, and includes a proprietary Accessibility Set for organisations needing compliance with anti-discrimination and W3C accessibility standards. Its Cyrillic and Greek were recognised with Granshan Awards and featured in Typographica's Best Typefaces of 2015.

name: New Atten
classification_notes: New Atten is explicitly described as combining a humanistic structure with classical capital proportions, making humanist sans the appropriate subcategory. Its rounded variant and text-optimisation features reinforce its warm, legibility-focused character rather than geometric or grotesque neutrality.
summary: A humanist sans with classical capital proportions and a rounded variant, designed with long-text legibility and a gently personable British character.
description: Named in reference to Sir David Attenborough's measured delivery, New Atten pairs a humanistic structure with classical capital proportions to sit comfortably at text sizes. The family runs across five weights with matching italics, and a Round variant adds mono-linear softness for extended reading. Accessibility features and alternate character sets, including infant a and g forms, make it suitable for children's publishing alongside general editorial and typographic work.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Atten as inspired by Sir David Attenborough's gentle, measured diction, which they saw as embodying the qualities of charm and personable tone balanced with textual integrity. The design evolved from an exploration of rounded typefaces into a humanistic sans with classical capital proportions optimised for long-text reading. New Atten Round is described as a mono-linear sans tailored for extended reading, with an Accessibility Set, Stylistic Set 2 offering infant character forms for children's books, and Stylistic Set 3 adding a minimal numeral 1 referencing Edward Johnston's London Transport typeface.

name: New Atten Round
classification_notes: A rounded monolinear sans with humanist structure and classical capital proportions, designed explicitly for extended text reading comfort while retaining warmth and approachability.
summary: A rounded humanist sans drawn from the measured authority of David Attenborough's voice, engineered for extended reading with accessibility features built in.
description: The design draws on the gentle, measured diction of Sir David Attenborough as an archetype for combining authority with warmth, aimed squarely at long-form text settings. Five weights from Book to Extra Bold are available in both upright and italic, with Stylistic Sets providing infant letterforms for children's publishing and a differentiating cap-I and lowercase-l pairing via an Accessibility Set. Newlyn claim it is the only rounded monolinear sans suited to extended reading, a bold position backed by its classical capital proportions and carefully tuned punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Atten Round as a rounded sans serif inspired by the voice of Sir David Attenborough, blending BBC-style authority with British warmth for clear, personable communications. The humanistic structure and classical capital proportions are intended to be timeless, and the family includes an Accessibility Set with differentiating letterforms, infant 'a' and 'g' via Stylistic Set 2 for children's books, and a minimal numeral 1 referencing Edward Johnston's London Transport typeface via Stylistic Set 3.

name: New Atten Italic
classification_notes: New Atten is described explicitly as combining a humanistic structure with classical capital proportions, making it a humanist sans serif. Its mono-linear rounded variant and text-optimised spacing reinforce this classification.
summary: A humanist sans serif with rounded variant named in tribute to Sir David Attenborough, designed for extended reading with accessibility features and alternate letterforms for children's books.
description: Combining a humanistic structure with classical capital proportions, New Atten is built for comfortable long-text reading across five weights from Book to Extra Bold, each with an italic. The Round subfamily takes a mono-linear approach with carefully tuned punctuation for text sizes, while an Accessibility Set improves legibility and Stylistic Set 2 offers infant a and g forms suited to children's publishing. A minimal numeral 1 drawn from Edward Johnston's London Underground lettering appears via Stylistic Set 3.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Atten as inspired by the gentle, measured voice of Sir David Attenborough, whose delivery embodied the charm and personable tone they sought in a typeface. Evolved from an exploration of rounded sans serifs, it pairs a humanistic structure with classical capital proportions to maximise reading comfort, with OpenType features including an Accessibility Set, infant letterforms for children's books, and a Johnston-influenced numeral 1.

name: New Atten Round Italic
classification_notes: A rounded monolinear sans with humanist structure and classical capital proportions, designed for extended reading comfort at text sizes — placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition with a rounded terminal treatment.
summary: The italic companion to New Atten Round, a rounded humanist sans designed for extended reading with an accessible character set and classical capital proportions.
description: Inspired by the measured delivery of Sir David Attenborough, New Atten Round Italic pairs rounded terminals with a humanist structure built for long-text comfort. The family includes an Accessibility Set with differentiating forms for capital I and lowercase l, plus stylistic sets offering infant a and g for children's book use, and a minimal numeral 1 referencing Edward Johnston's London Transport letterforms. Five weights run from Book to Extra Bold, each with matching italics across the New Atten collection.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Atten as a sans inspired by Sir David Attenborough, blending BBC-style authority with British warmth for clear, personable communications. They claim New Atten Round to be the only rounded monolinear sans suitable for extended reading, and highlight its humanistic structure, classical capital proportions, and Accessibility Set as making it suitable for everything from editorial work to children's books.

name: New Frank
classification_notes: New Frank is a grotesque sans-serif with condensed and compressed width variants, consistent with the industrial grotesque tradition. The sample copy referencing rail corporations reinforces a functional, signage-oriented grotesque lineage.
summary: A six-weight grotesque family from Newlyn with condensed and compressed subfamilies, suited to signage, transport and identity work.
description: New Frank spans six weights from Thin to ExtraBold, each with a matching italic, across three width variants: standard, Condensed and Compressed. The specimen copy references rail corporations from India, Malaysia, Norway and Brazil, pointing toward its intended use in wayfinding and institutional contexts. It is a clean, workmanlike grotesque without strong stylistic idiosyncrasy, but its range makes it practical for multi-format identity systems.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn present New Frank as a collection of 18 upright styles and matching italics across six weights, available in standard, Condensed and Compressed widths, with the family priced from $384.

name: New Frank Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed grotesque sans-serif, part of the New Frank collection alongside standard and compressed widths, following the proportions and conventions of 20th-century European grotesque typefaces.
summary: The condensed width of Newlyn's New Frank family, offering six weights with matching italics for space-efficient editorial and display applications.
description: New Frank Condensed sits in the middle of a three-width system alongside the standard and compressed cuts, covering Thin through ExtraBold in upright and italic. The proportions suit headline setting and space-constrained layouts without sacrificing legibility at text sizes. Twelve styles in total make it a flexible tool for editorial, identity and screen work.
foundry_blurb: New Frank Condensed is part of the New Frank collection from Newlyn, available in six weights from Thin to ExtraBold with matching italics. The collection spans three widths: standard, condensed and compressed, each in six styles.

name: New Frank Compressed
classification_notes: A compressed grotesque sans-serif, part of the New Frank collection that spans standard, condensed and compressed widths across six weights with matching italics.
summary: The most tightly drawn width of the New Frank family, a compressed grotesque running across six weights with matching italics.
description: New Frank Compressed sits at the narrowest extreme of the New Frank collection, which spans standard, condensed and compressed widths. Six weights from Thin to ExtraBold each carry a corresponding italic, giving 12 styles in total. The tight proportions suit poster work, headlines and contexts where horizontal economy is as important as typographic character.
foundry_blurb: New Frank Compressed is available as part of the New Frank collection, offered in three width variants — standard, condensed and compressed — each in six weights. The compressed family is priced from $384 for the full set of styles.

name: New Frank Italic
classification_notes: New Frank is a grotesque sans-serif with condensed and compressed variants, following the tradition of gothic/grotesque industrial type. The italic cuts reinforce its functional, workhorse character.
summary: The italic companion to New Frank, a grotesque sans available in six weights with condensed and compressed variants.
description: Matching the upright New Frank in six weights from Thin to ExtraBold, these italics complete a family that extends across three widths: standard, Condensed and Compressed. The specimen copy leans heavily on transport and rail operators as use-case references, suggesting the family is oriented towards signage, wayfinding and functional editorial work. Clean and unshowy, it sits comfortably in contexts where legibility across weights matters more than personality.
foundry_blurb: New Frank is offered as a collection spanning 6 weights across three subfamilies: New Frank, New Frank Condensed and New Frank Compressed, with full italic support throughout. The italic page showcases the slanted cuts across all six weights at both display and text sizes.

name: New Frank Compressed Italic
classification_notes: A compressed grotesque sans-serif, part of the wider New Frank collection that includes standard, condensed and compressed widths across multiple weights with italics throughout.
summary: A compressed grotesque cut from the New Frank family, offering six weights with matching italics for tight-set display and poster work.
description: New Frank Compressed is the narrowest of three width variants in the New Frank collection, sitting alongside the standard and Condensed cuts. Six weights run from Thin to ExtraBold, each paired with an italic, giving a total of twelve styles within the Compressed subfamily alone. The tight proportions and upright grotesque structure suit headline setting, arcade-style display work and any context where horizontal economy matters.
foundry_blurb: New Frank Compressed is part of the New Frank collection, available in three widths: New Frank, New Frank Condensed and New Frank Compressed. Each width comes in six weights from Thin to ExtraBold, with the full family buyable as a collection.

name: New Herman
classification_notes: New Herman presents as a grotesque sans-serif with four weights from Light to ExtraBold, following the conventions of European grotesque types with sturdy, upright forms suited to text and display use.
summary: A four-weight grotesque sans from Newlyn, spanning Light to ExtraBold for text and display applications.
description: New Herman is a grotesque sans-serif offered in four weights from Light through to ExtraBold, each available as OpenType fonts. The family reads cleanly at both small text sizes and large display settings, with specimen text shown across multiple European languages suggesting broad Latin character support. With no italic or condensed cuts, it is a compact, focused offering suited to straightforward editorial and branding work.
foundry_blurb: New Herman is available to buy as a family from Newlyn, with individual styles offered across four weights from Light to ExtraBold, each as OpenType fonts.

name: New Reason
classification_notes: Drawn directly from late 19th-century grotesques by Stephenson, Blake & Co., New Reason is a revival grotesque with wide, stable capitals and a narrower lowercase, placing it firmly in the Victorian grotesque lineage rather than the German or American branches.
summary: A revival grotesque by Miles Newlyn, drawn from Stephenson, Blake & Co. originals, with two character sets tuned for headings and text respectively.
description: The design preserves the characteristic tension between wide, stable capitals and a narrower, more animated lowercase found in late Victorian grotesques, while refining the rougher idiosyncrasies of its predecessors. Parentheses and mathematical symbols are redrawn as monolinear forms, improving readability, and tabular lining figures are highlighted as particularly suited to financial data. Five weights run from Light to Bold, and the dual character sets make it a practical choice for editorial and commercial work where warmth and period character are wanted without the roughness of direct historical revival.
foundry_blurb: Newlyn describe New Reason as a revival of late 19th-century grotesques — specifically those from Stephenson, Blake & Co. — made at a time before modernism had stripped such faces of their humanity. The design celebrates the bounce between capitals and lowercase, uniting wide, stable caps with a narrow, lively lower case. They frame it as an ode to sincere commerce and honest industry, and position it as warm, dignified and capable, with five weights and two character sets for headings and text.

name: New Rubrik
classification_notes: New Rubrik is a grotesque sans-serif with seven weights and matching italics, positioned for neutral professional and institutional use across a wide range of sizes.
summary: A fourteen-style grotesque sans in seven weights with matching italics, suited to institutional and corporate applications from text to display sizes.
description: Running from ExtraLight to ExtraBold with a full italic complement, New Rubrik is a clean grotesque designed to perform across a broad size range. The specimen leans heavily on institutional clients such as the Latvian National Opera, Norwegian Science Data Services and Ferrovie dello Stato Italiane, suggesting a positioning in public-sector and corporate identity. Spacing and weight progression are even, making it a capable workhorse where neutrality and legibility are the priority.
foundry_blurb: New Rubrik is offered in 7 weights with matching italics, giving a total of 14 styles. The family is positioned for versatile professional use, with specimen settings demonstrating performance at both text and display sizes across a range of institutional and corporate applications.

name: New Rubrik Italic
classification_notes: New Rubrik is a clean, geometric sans-serif with even stroke weights and neutral proportions typical of the grotesque-geometric hybrid tradition. Its upright and italic forms share the measured, corporate-ready character common to that classification.
summary: A geometric sans-serif in seven weights with matching italics, suited to institutional and editorial work across a range of sizes.
description: New Rubrik offers seven weights from ExtraLight to ExtraBold, each with a corresponding italic, giving a full 14-style family for text and display use. The letterforms are clean and neutral with even stroke contrast, making it well suited to branding and communications work for institutions, science bodies and financial organisations. Its proportions handle both large headline settings and smaller body copy without strain.
foundry_blurb: New Rubrik is available in 7 weights across both upright and italic styles, forming a 14-font family. Newlyn position it as a versatile geometric sans for a wide range of professional applications, from display settings down to running text.

name: New Rubrik Edge
classification_notes: New Rubrik Edge is a geometric sans-serif with clean, structured forms typical of the grotesque-geometric tradition. The 'Edge' designation suggests sharper, more angular detailing within the Rubrik family lineage.
summary: A seven-weight geometric sans from Newlyn's Rubrik family, distinguished by sharper edge detailing across upright and italic styles.
description: Part of Newlyn's broader Rubrik family, the Edge variant introduces more angular, defined forms across fourteen styles spanning ExtraLight to ExtraBold with matching italics. The range covers both text and display settings comfortably, with the weight spread making it adaptable for editorial and corporate applications. Clean construction and consistent geometry give it a neutral but considered character.
foundry_blurb: New Rubrik Edge is available in 7 weights with matching italics, offering 14 styles in total. The family is sold as a complete package and is available to try for free before purchase.

name: New Rubrik Edge Italic
classification_notes: New Rubrik Edge is a geometric sans-serif with sharp, angular detailing implied by the 'Edge' designation, offering a clean structural base across seven weights with matching italics.
summary: A geometric sans-serif in seven weights with matching italics, distinguished by sharp edge detailing that gives it a harder character than its Rubrik siblings.
description: Part of Newlyn's New Rubrik collection, this variant introduces angular edge detailing across ExtraLight through ExtraBold, each weight paired with a corresponding italic. The family suits editorial and branding contexts where a clean geometric structure is needed with a slightly harder, more technical edge. Fourteen styles in total are available as OpenType fonts.
foundry_blurb: New Rubrik Edge is offered in 7 weights from ExtraLight to ExtraBold, with a full matching italic family of 7 styles, making 14 fonts in total available to buy as a family from $384.

name: New Zen
classification_notes: The forms suggest a humanist sans with calligraphic underpinnings, evident in the optical proportions and the warmth carried across a broad weight range from Thin to ExtraBold.
summary: A humanist sans in 14 styles spanning seven weights from Thin to ExtraBold, with a matching italic series throughout.
description: A humanist sans from Newlyn offered in seven weights with full italic companions, making 14 styles in total. The specimen draws on Old Master and Baroque painting titles for its display usage, suggesting a sensibility suited to cultural and editorial contexts. At text sizes the forms hold their composure across the weight range without undue personality.
foundry_blurb: New Zen is offered as a collection of 14 styles: seven upright weights from Thin to ExtraBold and seven matching italics, available to buy as a family from $384.

name: New Zen Italic
classification_notes: The display samples and text behaviour suggest a refined transitional serif with upright and italic companions; the italic subfamily is marketed as a distinct but paired companion to New Zen's seven-weight roman range.
summary: A seven-weight italic companion to New Zen, designed for use alongside the roman in editorial and text settings.
description: New Zen Italic spans seven weights from Thin to ExtraBold, mirroring the structure of the roman family and intended for use in combination with it. The specimen leans on Old Master painting titles and Latin text to demonstrate legibility across display and text sizes. Sold as part of the broader New Zen collection, which totals fourteen styles across the two subfamilies.
foundry_blurb: New Zen is a collection of 14 styles comprising seven weights of the roman and seven weights of the italic, priced from $384 for the full family.

name: New Farm
classification_notes: New Farm is built around a typewriter-monospaced grid but with handwritten, bouncy letterforms, wide proportions and rounded terminals, making it a humanist mono rather than a strict mechanical one.
summary: A monospaced typeface with handwritten roots, built around springy stems, rounded terminals and wide proportions for a relaxed, upbeat character.
description: Designed by Miles Newlyn and Ben Mitchell for a client brief calling for something upbeat and unpretentious, New Farm grafts lively, handwritten shapes onto a typewriter-monospaced structure. Raindrop-shaped counters, rounded terminals and wide proportions give it a casual warmth that stops well short of novelty, with bounce calibrated so that letters settle into a regular rhythm. The family spans five weights from Thin to Bold and includes Newlyn's Accessibility Set for organisations with compliance requirements.
foundry_blurb: New Farm started as a commissioned design for a client seeking an upbeat, relaxed and unpretentious typeface. Newlyn built it from simple handwritten shapes, then refined the degree of bounce through testing to arrive at springy stems, rounded terminals, wide proportions and raindrop-shaped counters within a typewriter-monospaced structure. The result is described as funky and carefree but tempered enough to hold a regular rhythm. Like all Newlyn typefaces, it ships with the Accessibility Set feature.

name: New Frank Condensed Italic
classification_notes: New Frank Condensed is a condensed grotesque sans-serif, part of a wider family spanning regular, condensed and compressed widths across six weights with matching italics.
summary: A condensed grotesque sans offered in six weights with matching italics, forming part of the broader New Frank collection from Newlyn.
description: New Frank Condensed sits within a three-width family alongside the standard and compressed cuts, each available in six weights from Thin to ExtraBold. The condensed proportion suits tighter editorial columns, headlines and display settings where vertical economy matters. Italic styles accompany all six weights, giving the family considerable range for mixed typographic hierarchies.
foundry_blurb: New Frank Condensed is offered as part of the New Frank collection, which spans regular, condensed and compressed widths each in six styles. The family is available to buy or try for free, with pricing starting from $384 for the full family.
